summary: Excellent C‑Miller soundboard capture of a hot 1984 show with standout Samson/Delilah, Ship of Fools and an epic Playing in the Band → China Doll jam.
review: All five reviewers rate the set four stars and rave about the crisp, well‑balanced Miller‑remastered soundboard tape. Musically the concert is described as a solid first set that blossoms into a spectacular second set highlighted by Samson & Delilah, Ship of Fools, a powerful Playing in the Band → China Doll sequence and superb improvisations throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 4‑star scores, enthusiastic language about both performance and recording quality; no major complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: C. Miller remaster praised as crisp, clear and well balanced; no drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > China Doll
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as good and focused; second set deemed even better with a relaxed, jam‑heavy vibe that builds to a long Space and a fiery finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia's solos called "peel the paint off your walls", his vocals on Ship of Fools praised as stellar, and his guitar work described as high‑energy throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil's bass highlighted for "whale songs" and serious funk grooves in the second set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob noted for a more restrained rhythm part on Rooster; one reviewer mentions occasional tuning issues but no major criticism.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland praised repeatedly – fine jazz keys, harpsichord texture on China Doll, solid B3 work and overall strong keyboard presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann's high cymbals noted; drummers (Bill & Mickey) credited with funk foundation and tight transitions into Space.
